Name: Tangela
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Tangrowth
Flavor-Text: Vine growth is accelerated for Tangrowth living in warm climates. If the vines grow long, Tangrowth shortens them by tearing parts of them off.

Name: Heracross
Effects: If you have any Stage 2 Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

Name: Celebi
Ability-Effect: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon traveled through time to come from the future. It bolsters grass and trees with its own strength, and it can heal wounds, too.

Name: Treecko
Flavor-Text: Treecko can quickly scale even vertical surfaces. It senses humidity with its tail to predict the next day’s weather.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Grovyle leaps from branch to branch in large trees, moving with ease. It propels itself with astounding speed and force.

Name: Sceptile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It agilely flits through the jungle and uses the sharp-edged leaves on its arms to cut down its prey.

Name: Mega Sceptile ex
Effects: Discard Grass[G] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It secretes a thick, sweet-scented syrup from the tip of its head. It lives on weed-choked ponds.

Name: Masquerain
Flavor-Text: The antennae have distinctive patterns that look like eyes. When it rains, they grow heavy, making flight impossible.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scatters spores from the top of its head to protect itself.

Name: Breloom
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The seeds on its tail are made of toxic spores. It knocks out foes with quick, virtually invisible punches.

Name: Budew
Effects: The Defending Pokémon loses all Abilities. This effect lasts until the Defending Pokémon leaves the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is highly sensitive to temperature changes. When its bud starts to open, that means spring is right around the corner.

Name: Carnivine
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Sewaddle
Flavor-Text: The bumps on their heads are sensory organs. When Sewaddle meet, they greet each other by rubbing these bumps together.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Swadloon wraps itself in leaves for protection. It uses the two leaves atop its head to detect air movements and feel out its surroundings.

Name: Leavanny
Flavor-Text: This gentle Pokémon has strong protective instincts. The leaves on its arms are sharp enough to slice a thick tree in half with one stroke.

Name: Durant
Effects: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

Name: Applin
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Flapple
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: It uses its own body fluid to repair its apple. Strong Flapple that have won many battles have apples that are clay colored all over.

Name: Numel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The flaming magma it stores in the hump on its back is the source of its tremendous power.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each [R] Energy you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The volcanoes on its back have a major eruption every 10 years—or whenever it becomes really angry.

Name: Mega Camerupt ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random. Do 160 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Castform Sunny Form
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Castform changes to this form when it basks in bright sunlight. When you touch its glowing skin, it feels all dried out!

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack of 1 of your [R] Pokémon, you may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can't use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Tepig
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, take 2 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a ravenous glutton. It uses its excellent sense of smell to find food, then cooks it to a crisp before eating.

Name: Pignite
Flavor-Text: When defending its friends, Pignite will stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong. But it’s powerless when faced with an empty stomach.

Name: Emboar
Effects: Flip a coin for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

Name: Darumaka
Flavor-Text: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The thick arms of this hot-blooded Pokémon can deliver punches capable of obliterating a dump truck.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: In ancient times, Larvesta was worshiped as the emissary of the sun. However, it was also viewed as a burden since it often caused forest fires.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scatters burning scales. Most of the danger of these scales is not in their heat—it’s in the way they rob the surrounding air of oxygen.

Name: Poliwag
Flavor-Text: Its legs are newly grown, and it can’t walk very well. It seems to prefer swimming through the water instead.

Name: Poliwhirl
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is moist all over its body. The skin on its belly spiral also feels smooth.

Name: Politoed
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Poliwhirl during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s the leader of Poliwag and Poliwhirl. When Politoed roars, they all cower in fear.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Flavor-Text: It swims by jetting water from its horns. The most notable characteristic of the Aqua Breed is its high body fat, which allows it to float easily.

Name: Vaporeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Wooper
Flavor-Text: When it walks around on land, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [F] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on riverbed boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

Name: Castform Rainy Form
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This is Castform’s form when pelted by rain. In an experiment where it was placed in a shower, this Pokémon didn’t change to this form.

Name: Castform Snowy Form
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This is Castform’s form when caught in a hailstorm. Its whole body is chilled, and its skin is partially frozen!

Name: Clamperl
Flavor-Text: Clamperl’s pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder’s pearls.

Name: Huntail
Flavor-Text: Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.

Name: Gorebyss
Flavor-Text: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.

Name: Regice
Effects: Move 2 random Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s body is made of solid ice. It’s said that Regice was born beneath thick ice in the ice age.

Name: Cubchoo
Flavor-Text: When Cubchoo starts sneezing, watch out! If it spatters you with its frosty snot, you’ll get frostbite.

Name: Beartic
Flavor-Text: Feared as the Snow-White Demon in northern lands, Beartic uses its frosty claws and fangs to attack prey.

Name: Sobble
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It’s a very cautious Pokémon. When it has no choice but to battle, it hides itself before attacking.

Name: Drizzile
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may do 20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it defends its home by laying traps.

Name: Inteleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may do 30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It may present itself as being well-mannered, but deep down, it still has a lazy side. It will slack off when its Trainer isn’t looking.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [W] and [F] type.
Effects: Discard a [W] Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in defeating foes by raining many blows down on them. Its strikes are nonstop, flowing like a river.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges electromagnetic waves and so on from the units at its sides.

Name: Magneton
Flavor-Text: They’re formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Magnezone ex
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Chinchou
Flavor-Text: It shoots positively and negatively charged electricity from the tips of its two antennae and stuns its opponents.

Name: Lanturn
Flavor-Text: It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mythology tells us that if people lose the righteousness in their hearts, their kingdoms will be razed by Zekrom’s lightning.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The jolts of electricity it launches by violently strumming the protrusions on its chest easily exceed 15,000 volts.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a small stomach. If it isn’t constantly eating the seeds it keeps in its pockets, it will get hungry immediately.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

Name: Espurr
Flavor-Text: Behind an Espurr’s expressionless face is a frantic struggle to contain psychic power.

Name: Meowstic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Confused.
Flavor-Text: If Meowstic continuously emits its powerful psychic energy, it will damage its own body.

Name: Diancie
Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your [P] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

Name: Hatenna
Flavor-Text: It senses the feelings of other living creatures. Be careful not to expose it to strong emotions for too long, or it will end up exhausted.

Name: Hattrem
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The moment this Pokémon finds someone who’s emitting strong emotions, it will pummel them senseless with its braids to silence them.

Name: Hatterene
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Beware of forests that show no signs of living creatures within. You may have wandered into Hatterene’s territory.

Name: Bramblin
Flavor-Text: Not even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.

Name: Brambleghast
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may move 30 damage that your Active Pokémon has on it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It glides as if sliding. It startles foes by clamping on to their faces, then jabs with its poison stinger.

Name: Gliscor
Flavor-Text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Trapinch
Flavor-Text: As it digs through the sand, its giant jaws crush any rocks that obstruct its path. It builds a funnel-shaped nest.

Name: Regirock
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Cutting-edge technology was used to study the internals of this Pokémon’s rock body, but nothing was found—not even a brain or a heart.

Name: Bonsly
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −30 damage.
Flavor-Text: From its eyes, it can expel excess moisture from its body. This liquid is similar in composition to human sweat.

Name: Riolu
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions, such as joy and rage, in the form of waves.

Name: Lucario
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes +20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Croagunk
Flavor-Text: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.

Name: Gallade
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

Name: Throh
Effects: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It performs throwing moves with first-rate skill. Over the course of many battles, Throh’s belt grows darker as it absorbs its wearer’s sweat.

Name: Sawk
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The karate chops of a Sawk that’s trained itself to the limit can cleave the ocean itself.

Name: Dwebble
Flavor-Text: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

Name: Crustle ex
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

Name: Bewear
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

Name: Rolycoly
Flavor-Text: Chunks of the surface of this Pokémon’s body that have grown old and flaked off have long been used for fuel as an alternative to coal.

Name: Carkol
Flavor-Text: Due to the coal tar created inside it, the heap of coal on Carkol’s back never falls apart, even when the Pokémon rolls around at high speeds.

Name: Coalossal
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: To intimidate its opponents, Coalossal will vigorously shake its body, scattering coal from its smoldering back.

Name: Silicobra
Flavor-Text: Silicobra’s large nostrils are specialized for spraying sand, so this Pokémon is not very good at telling apart different smells.

Name: Sandaconda
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: The sand it spews contains sharp, pointy gravel, which is actually just sand that Sandaconda’s saliva has hardened into irregular shapes.

Name: Kubfu
Effects: Take a [C] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun’s light won’t reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

Name: Golbat
Flavor-Text: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

Name: Crobat
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flaps its four wings skillfully. Crobat can fly through cramped caves without needing to slow down.

Name: Koffing
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

Name: Weezing
Flavor-Text: When it inhales poisonous gases from garbage, its body expands, and its insides smell much worse.

Name: Qwilfish
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Experienced fishers say they try to catch Qwilfish in the brief moment that these Pokémon become defenseless just after launching poisonous spikes.

Name: Seviper
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It’s Zangoose’s archrival.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Zoroark ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [D] Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Foongus
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon prefers damp places. It spurts out poison spores to repel approaching enemies.

Name: Amoonguss
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Amoonguss mimics Poké Balls. It is not yet clear whether this mimicry is effective against other Pokémon.

Name: Vullaby
Flavor-Text: Its healthy appetite leads to visible growth spurts. It often has to replace the bones it wears as its size increases.

Name: Mandibuzz
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that have damage on them.
Flavor-Text: Watching from the sky, they attack weakened prey on the ground. They have a habit of decorating themselves with bones.

Name: Inkay
Flavor-Text: Being upside-down improves its blood flow and clears its mind, causing the power of its psychic moves to increase dramatically.

Name: Malamar
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the luminescent spots on its body to hypnotize its opponents, gaining total control over them.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [F] and [D] type.
Effects: Discard a [D] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist.

Name: Zarude
Effects: If any of your [D] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

Name: Bombirdier
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active [D] Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 1 less.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

Name: Registeel
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It’s rumored that this Pokémon was born deep underground in the planet’s mantle and that it emerged onto the surface 10,000 years ago.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: They are found in ancient tombs. The patterns on their backs are said to be imbued with mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: If your opponent has any [P] Pokémon in play, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bisharp mercilessly cuts its opponents to pieces with the sharp blades covering its body. It will do anything to win.

Name: Magearna
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.

Name: Melmetal
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +60 damage.
Flavor-Text: Centrifugal force is behind the punches of Melmetal’s heavy hex-nut arms. Melmetal is said to deliver the strongest punches of all Pokémon.

Name: Corviknight ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It generates ultrasonic waves by vigorously vibrating its wings. After making its prey faint, it dissolves the prey with its digestive juices.

Name: Flygon ex
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Blissey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may remove a random Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a Blissey finds a weakened Pokémon, it will share its egg and offer its care until the other Pokémon is all better.

Name: Eevee
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: If spotted, it escapes by burrowing with its tail. It can float just slightly using its wings.

Name: Teddiursa
Flavor-Text: Its paws are soaked in oodles of honey. When nervous, Teddiursa will lick its paws and soon have a smile back on its face.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: This attack does more damage equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text: It is quite skilled at climbing trees. If it comes across a Primeape while searching for berries in the treetops, trouble will surely ensue.

Name: Castform
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!

Name: Regigigas
Ability-Effect: If you don't have Regirock, Regice, and Registeel on your Bench, this Pokémon can't attack.
Effects: Take a [C] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

Name: Patrat
Flavor-Text: Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on the lookout, but it won’t notice a foe coming from behind.

Name: Watchog
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Psych Up attack does +30 damage.
Flavor-Text: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is far brighter than the average child, and Lillipup won’t forget the love it receives or any abuse it suffers.

Name: Herdier
Flavor-Text: The black fur that covers this Pokémon’s body is dense and springy. Even sharp fangs bounce right off.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive moustache. It’s said that moustache length is what determines social standing among this species.

Name: Audino
Effects: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions.

Name: Mega Audino ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon like things neat and tidy. They are always sweeping and dusting their habitat, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The oil that seeps from its body is really smooth. For people troubled by bad skin, this oil is an effective treatment.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Rookidee
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The females are fussier than the males. If another creature dirties a female Rookidee’s wings, it’ll peck the offender relentlessly in a burning rage.

Name: Corvisquire
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon will quickly learn how to use any tool it can hold in its beak or its talons.

Name: Field Blower
Description: Discard a Pokémon Tool card from a Pokémon (yours or your opponent's), or discard a Stadium card in play.

Name: Lucky Egg
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

Name: Korrina
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex.

Name: Cabbie
Description: Put a random Stadium card from your deck into your hand.

Name: Cheren
Description: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Watchog and Stoutland take −100 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

Name: Parasol Lady
Description: Put 1 of your [W] Pokémon in play, except any Pokémon ex, into your hand.

Name: Fragrant Forest
Description: Once during each player's turn, that player may put a random Basic [G] Pokémon from their deck into their hand.

Name: Arena of Antiquity
Description: Attacks used by each [F] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) do +20 damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon ex.

Name: Bounded Field
Description: When applying the opponent's Active Pokémon's Weakness to damage from attacks used by Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) that aren't Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, apply Weakness as ×2.

Name: Treecko
Flavor-Text: Treecko can quickly scale even vertical surfaces. It senses humidity with its tail to predict the next day’s weather.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Grovyle leaps from branch to branch in large trees, moving with ease. It propels itself with astounding speed and force.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scatters spores from the top of its head to protect itself.

Name: Budew
Effects: The Defending Pokémon loses all Abilities. This effect lasts until the Defending Pokémon leaves the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is highly sensitive to temperature changes. When its bud starts to open, that means spring is right around the corner.

Name: Flapple
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: It uses its own body fluid to repair its apple. Strong Flapple that have won many battles have apples that are clay colored all over.

Name: Tepig
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, take 2 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a ravenous glutton. It uses its excellent sense of smell to find food, then cooks it to a crisp before eating.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [F] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on riverbed boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mythology tells us that if people lose the righteousness in their hearts, their kingdoms will be razed by Zekrom’s lightning.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a small stomach. If it isn’t constantly eating the seeds it keeps in its pockets, it will get hungry immediately.

Name: Meowstic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Confused.
Flavor-Text: If Meowstic continuously emits its powerful psychic energy, it will damage its own body.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

Name: Brambleghast
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may move 30 damage that your Active Pokémon has on it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.

Name: Bonsly
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −30 damage.
Flavor-Text: From its eyes, it can expel excess moisture from its body. This liquid is similar in composition to human sweat.

Name: Riolu
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions, such as joy and rage, in the form of waves.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The melodies sung by Meloetta have the power to make Pokémon that hear them happy or sad.

Name: Carkol
Flavor-Text: Due to the coal tar created inside it, the heap of coal on Carkol’s back never falls apart, even when the Pokémon rolls around at high speeds.

Name: Kubfu
Effects: Take a [C] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.

Name: Seviper
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It’s Zangoose’s archrival.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Malamar
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the luminescent spots on its body to hypnotize its opponents, gaining total control over them.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bisharp mercilessly cuts its opponents to pieces with the sharp blades covering its body. It will do anything to win.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.

Name: Castform
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The oil that seeps from its body is really smooth. For people troubled by bad skin, this oil is an effective treatment.

Name: Mega Sceptile ex
Effects: Discard Grass[G] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Camerupt ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random. Do 160 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Vaporeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Magnezone ex
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Crustle ex
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Zoroark ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [D] Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Corviknight ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Flygon ex
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Audino ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Korrina
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex.

Name: Cabbie
Description: Put a random Stadium card from your deck into your hand.

Name: Cheren
Description: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Watchog and Stoutland take −100 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

Name: Parasol Lady
Description: Put 1 of your [W] Pokémon in play, except any Pokémon ex, into your hand.

Name: Mega Sceptile ex
Effects: Discard Grass[G] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Camerupt ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random. Do 160 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Vaporeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Magnezone ex
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Crustle ex
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Zoroark ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched [D] Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Corviknight ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Flygon ex
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Audino ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Sobble
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It’s a very cautious Pokémon. When it has no choice but to battle, it hides itself before attacking.

Name: Mega Lucario ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Cottonee
Flavor-Text: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

Name: Torchic
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Combusken
Flavor-Text: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

Name: Blaziken
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

Name: Frillish
Flavor-Text: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

Name: Jellicent
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

Name: Onix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun’s light won’t reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

Name: Golbat
Flavor-Text: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

Name: Crobat
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flaps its four wings skillfully. Crobat can fly through cramped caves without needing to slow down.

Name: Grimer
Flavor-Text: Appears in filthy areas. It thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

Name: Muk
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: They love to gather in smelly areas where sludge accumulates, making the stench around them worse.

Name: Absol
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.
Flavor-Text: Because of this Pokémon’s ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

Name: Skrelp
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a poor swimmer. If it’s caught in a fierce storm, it will sometimes get washed far away and become unable to return to its home.

Name: Steelix
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Blissey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may remove a random Special Condition from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a Blissey finds a weakened Pokémon, it will share its egg and offer its care until the other Pokémon is all better.

Name: Porygon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

Name: Porygon2
Flavor-Text: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Trainer card in your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Blaziken ex
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Greninja ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active [W] Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Jolteon ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy from their Energy Zone to 1 of their Pokémon, do 20 damage to that Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Hitmonchan ex
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Absol ex
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and discard it.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Dragalge ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Steelix ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks and has no Weakness.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Celebi
Ability-Effect: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon traveled through time to come from the future. It bolsters grass and trees with its own strength, and it can heal wounds, too.

Name: Bombirdier
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active [D] Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 1 less.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.
